abstract: The *ortho*-substituted phenyl ring is a fundamental structural motif in modern chemistry, featured in over three hundred commercial drugs and agrochemicals. Over the last decade, there has been a significant push to replace aromatic rings in bioactive molecules with saturated bioisosteres to discover novel, patentable chemical space. Despite this interest, efforts have largely focused on the replacement of *para*-substituted rings, leaving a gap in the design of *ortho* mimics. In this work, we present 2-oxabicyclo[2.1.1]hexanes as saturated bioisosteres for the *ortho*-substituted phenyl ring. Crystallographic analysis confirms that these bicyclic scaffolds closely mirror the geometric properties of their aromatic counterparts. By replacing the phenyl rings in the commercial fungicides fluxapyroxad and boscalid with these bioisosteres, we demonstrate that the resulting analogues exhibit drastically improved water solubility and reduced lipophilicity while crucially maintaining their biological activity. These findings offer a robust strategy for medicinal and agrochemical researchers to optimize bioactive leads via the bioisosteric replacement of *ortho*-substituted phenyl motifs.
